message:
test(ftui): add PTY E2E scenario pack with headless multi-step user journeys (FTUI-07.3)

Adds 19 E2E tests exercising full model.update() pipeline for realistic
terminal sessions: view tours (Tab + digit navigation), cross-view workflows,
filter interactions (Events/History/Search), resize stress (10 sizes x 7 views),
key storm stress (rapid random input), degraded data states, long filter
strings (200 chars), and data refresh during active interaction. Includes
E2eSession helper with frame capture history and diagnostic dump for failure
artifacts. Also fixes render_search_view, render_events_view, and
render_history_view call signatures after LavenderGrove's FocusRegion additions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"wa-2i6m","title":"FTUI-04.2.a Adapter fixture pack for pane/event/triage/history mappings","description":"## Background
Adapter correctness depends on stable fixture inputs and expected outputs for pane/event/triage/history data shapes.

## Deliverables
- fixture corpus covering normal, missing, redacted, and malformed input variants
- expected mapped view-model outputs with stable ordering and deterministic serialization

## Acceptance Criteria
- fixtures cover all mapped domains (pane/event/triage/history) and high-risk edge cases
- failures emit detailed diagnostics suitable for CI logs and local triage
- harness integrates with unit-test suite and is referenced by downstream view parity tasks.,","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:14:13.884502410Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-08T21:30:36.578324037Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["adapter","ftui","subtask"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-2i6m","depends_on_id":"wa-33fn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:24:54.967794156Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"},{"issue_id":"wa-2i6m","depends_on_id":"wa-3kal","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:14:13.900466815Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"}]}
 {"id":"wa-2ji","title":"Stress test: scalability with 50+ panes and large transcripts","description":"# Task: Scalability Stress Tests

## Goal
Validate wa performance and stability under high load conditions.

## Test Scenarios

### 1. Many Panes (50+)
```bash
# Setup: Create 50 panes with concurrent activity
for i in {1..50}; do
    wezterm cli spawn --new-window -- bash -c "while true; do echo line $i; sleep 0.1; done" &
done

# Run watcher and measure:
wa watch &
sleep 60

# Metrics to collect:
# - CPU usage (should stay < 50%)
# - Memory usage (should stay < 500MB)
# - Ingest lag (should stay < 100ms)
# - Queue depth (should not grow unbounded)
```

### 2. Large Transcript (1M+ lines)
```bash
# Generate large output in single pane
for i in {1..1000000}; do
    echo "Log line $i with some content to match patterns against"
done

# Verify:
# - FTS indexing completes
# - Search remains responsive (< 500ms for broad query)
# - Memory usage bounded
# - No disk space explosion
```

### 3. High Event Rate
```bash
# Trigger many pattern matches rapidly
for i in {1..1000}; do
    echo "ERROR: test error $i"
    sleep 0.01
done

# Verify:
# - Event deduplication works
# - No event queue overflow
# - Notifications throttled appropriately
```

### 4. Long-Running Stability (24h)
```bash
# Run watcher for extended period with normal activity
wa watch &
# ... normal usage for 24 hours ...

# Check for:
# - Memory leaks (memory growth over time)
# - File handle leaks
# - Database size growth rate
# - No crashes or hangs
```

## Metrics Collection
```rust
struct StressTestMetrics {
    cpu_usage_percent: Vec<f32>,
    memory_mb: Vec<f32>,
    ingest_lag_ms: Vec<f32>,
    queue_depth: Vec<usize>,
    events_per_second: f32,
    fts_query_p99_ms: f32,
}
```

## Pass Criteria
| Scenario | Metric | Budget |
|----------|--------|--------|
| 50 panes | CPU idle | < 50% |
| 50 panes | Memory | < 500MB |
| 50 panes | Ingest lag | < 100ms |
| 1M lines | FTS query | < 500ms |
| 1M lines | Memory | < 1GB |
| 24h run | Memory growth | < 10% |
| 24h run | Crashes | 0 |

## Testing
- CI integration: run scaled-down version (10 panes, 100k lines)
- Manual: full stress test before releases
- Artifacts: metrics graphs, memory profiles

## Acceptance Criteria
- Stress test suite exists and is runnable
- Budget thresholds are enforced
- Regressions are visible in CI
- Long-running test detects leaks

-{"id":"wa-2q5r","title":"FTUI-01.1.a ADR: one-writer rule adaptation and terminal ownership rationale","description":"## Background
FrankenTUI's one-writer terminal ownership model must be translated into wa-specific runtime boundaries before implementation tasks proceed.

## Deliverables
- explicit mapping from ftui ownership principles to wa modules/process boundaries
- decision table for allowed/forbidden write paths during UI-active and command-handoff states
- risk notes for migration edge cases (panic path, subprocess path, inline mode)

## Acceptance Criteria
- mapping is concrete enough to serve as an implementation contract for downstream runtime tasks
- at least one negative example per forbidden write path is documented
- document includes required evidence fields for later unit/E2E validation and logging checks.,","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:13:51.443783314Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-08T21:30:31.857946433Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["adr","ftui","subtask"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-2q5r","depends_on_id":"wa-2dlw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:13:51.475021131Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"}]}

 {"id":"wa-2qyt","title":"FTUI-03.1 Introduce terminal session ownership abstraction aligned with ftui","description":"## Background\nCurrent TUI setup/teardown is tied to crossterm/ratatui lifecycle. We need a migration-safe ownership abstraction that matches ftui terminal session discipline.\n\n## Deliverables\n- terminal session lifecycle interface\n- raw mode / alt-screen / cleanup ownership model\n- integration points for command handoff and return\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- session ownership is singular and testable\n- teardown guarantees are explicit and verified.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"LavenderGrove","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:07:49.283003308Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-09T01:27:27.521898614Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T01:27:27.521835436Z","close_reason":"Implemented terminal_session module: TerminalSession trait, SessionPhase enum, SessionGuard RAII, CrosstermSession impl, MockTerminalSession. 12 tests. Clippy clean.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-2qyt","depends_on_id":"wa-1brb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:07:49.299737512Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"},{"issue_id":"wa-2qyt","depends_on_id":"wa-8q4e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:16:19.162217282Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"}]}
 {"id":"wa-2sfj","title":"FTUI-05.5.a Triage ranking and action parity checklist","description":"## Background
Triage behavior directly affects operator prioritization; ranking/action semantics must stay stable after migration.

## Deliverables
- parity checklist for ranking order, tie-breaking, action affordances, and state transitions
- deterministic test scenarios for changing queue composition and repeated refreshes
- evidence references to logs/snapshots demonstrating decision transparency

## Acceptance Criteria
- ranking and action behavior are reproducible and match expected policy semantics
- deviations are documented with impact assessment and mitigation path
- artifacts include unit-level assertions plus end-to-end validation traces.,","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:14:24.032266454Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-08T21:32:38.734461194Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","subtask","views"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-2sfj","depends_on_id":"wa-1ncy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:14:24.063939318Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"},{"issue_id":"wa-2sfj","depends_on_id":"wa-2i6m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:32:24.783189622Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":280,"issue_id":"wa-2sfj","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Canonicalization note: removed dependency on `wa-3akp` (Events parity checklist) and replaced with direct dependency on `wa-2i6m` (adapter fixture pack).

Rationale: triage-ranking parity should be gated by canonical fixture/evidence infrastructure, not serialized behind Events-specific parity validation. This preserves evidence consistency while enabling parallel execution of view-specific checklist work.","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:32:38Z"}]}
 {"id":"wa-2tk","title":"Metrics export: Prometheus endpoint with counters, gauges, histograms","description":"# Metrics Export

## Purpose
Expose Prometheus-compatible metrics for monitoring and alerting.

## Implementation

### Metrics Registry
```rust
use prometheus::{Registry, Counter, CounterVec, Gauge, GaugeVec, Histogram, HistogramVec};

lazy_static\! {
    pub static ref REGISTRY: Registry = Registry::new();
    
    // Counters
    pub static ref EVENTS_DETECTED: CounterVec = register_counter_vec\!(
        "wa_events_detected_total",
        "Total events detected",
        &["type", "agent"]
    ).unwrap();
    
    pub static ref WORKFLOWS_COMPLETED: CounterVec = register_counter_vec\!(
        "wa_workflows_completed_total",
        "Total workflows completed",
        &["workflow", "status"]
    ).unwrap();
    
    pub static ref POLICY_DECISIONS: CounterVec = register_counter_vec\!(
        "wa_policy_decisions_total",
        "Total policy decisions",
        &["decision", "action"]
    ).unwrap();
    
    // Gauges
    pub static ref PANES_OBSERVED: Gauge = register_gauge\!(
        "wa_panes_observed",
        "Number of observed panes"
    ).unwrap();
    
    pub static ref QUEUE_DEPTH: Gauge = register_gauge\!(
        "wa_queue_depth",
        "Current event queue depth"
    ).unwrap();
    
    // Histograms
    pub static ref PATTERN_MATCH_DURATION: Histogram = register_histogram\!(
        "wa_pattern_match_duration_seconds",
        "Time to run pattern matching",
        vec\![0.0001, 0.0005, 0.001, 0.005, 0.01, 0.05, 0.1]
    ).unwrap();
}
```

### HTTP Endpoint
```rust
use axum::{Router, routing::get};
use prometheus::Encoder;

async fn metrics_handler() -> impl IntoResponse {
    let encoder = prometheus::TextEncoder::new();
    let metric_families = REGISTRY.gather();
    let mut buffer = Vec::new();
    encoder.encode(&metric_families, &mut buffer).unwrap();
    (
        [(header::CONTENT_TYPE, "text/plain; charset=utf-8")],
        buffer,
    )
}

pub fn metrics_router() -> Router {
    Router::new().route("/metrics", get(metrics_handler))
}
```

### Metric Instrumentation Points
- Event detection: increment counter, record duration
- Workflow execution: increment counter by status
- Policy decisions: increment by decision type
- Pane changes: update gauge
- Pattern matching: record histogram

## Metrics Catalog

| Metric | Type | Labels | Description |
|--------|------|--------|-------------|
| wa_events_detected_total | counter | type, agent | Events detected |
| wa_workflows_completed_total | counter | workflow, status | Workflow completions |
| wa_policy_decisions_total | counter | decision, action | Policy evaluations |
| wa_panes_observed | gauge | - | Active panes |
| wa_queue_depth | gauge | - | Pending events |
| wa_db_size_bytes | gauge | - | Database size |
| wa_pattern_match_duration_seconds | histogram | - | Pattern match time |
| wa_workflow_step_duration_seconds | histogram | workflow, step | Step execution time |

## Testing
- Unit: Metrics increment correctly
- Integration: Endpoint serves valid Prometheus format
- E2E: Metrics change during workflow execution

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All core operations instrumented
- [ ] /metrics endpoint serves Prometheus format
- [ ] Cardinality kept reasonable (bounded labels)
- [ ] Histograms have sensible buckets
- [ ] Feature-flagged for minimal deployments

 {"id":"wa-2wed","title":"[EPIC] FrankenTUI Adoption Program for wa (across-the-board migration)","description":"## Background
`wa` currently has a feature-gated TUI built around `ratatui`/`crossterm` (`crates/wa-core/src/tui`). We now want to standardize on `/dp/frankentui` (ftui) across the project’s interactive terminal surfaces.

FrankenTUI contributes a stricter kernel model:
- one-writer rule for terminal ownership
- deterministic render loop (buffer/diff/presenter)
- inline-first behavior for scrollback safety
- explicit operational quality gates (PTY E2E, snapshot and perf baselines)

## Program Goal
Migrate wa’s TUI architecture and operator workflows to ftui across the board, with a staged rollout that preserves behavior parity, improves determinism, and removes ratatui-centric technical debt.

## Scope
- dependency/build integration with `/dp/frankentui`
- runtime/output ownership model migration
- screen-by-screen UI migration
- command/input parity migration
- testing/perf/compatibility gates
- documentation, rollout, and old-stack decommissioning plan

## Non-Goals
- No broad rewrite of robot/MCP/storage logic unrelated to UI migration.
- No speculative feature expansion before parity and reliability gates are met.

## Success Criteria
- wa TUI experience runs on ftui with feature parity (or explicit intentional deltas).
- deterministic tests and PTY E2E prove stability under sustained output and resize/input stress.
- migration docs are self-contained enough that future contributors do not need to read old planning docs.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:07:03.398731926Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-08T21:32:43.602721861Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","migration","program","tui"],"comments":[{"id":267,"issue_id":"wa-2wed","author":"GrayHarbor","text":"### FTUI Planning Notes (Self-Contained)
Source assumptions captured from `/dp/frankentui`:
- one-writer rule is a hard invariant (UI runtime owns terminal writes)
- deterministic rendering/state transitions are required for stable tests
- migration must be evidence-driven (parity matrices, PTY E2E artifacts, perf baselines)
- rollout must include explicit rollback triggers and decommission gates

This epic tree is designed so future contributors can execute without consulting the original planning markdown. Each FTUI-* issue includes scope, deliverables, and acceptance criteria. Dependencies encode intended execution order and prevent parallel drift.

Evidence expectations:
- parity evidence for each migrated surface
- deterministic test artifacts for lifecycle/input/resize/output stress
- compatibility/perf reports before go/no-go cutover","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:25:53Z"},{"id":282,"issue_id":"wa-2wed","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Execution contract update (frankentui across the board):
- Every FTUI implementation bead must ship explicit unit-test coverage and deterministic evidence artifacts.
- Every FTUI integration/cutover bead must include PTY E2E scenario evidence plus concise, redaction-safe logs sufficient for CI and post-failure triage.
- Any intentional parity delta must be logged in the parity ledger with user-impact rationale and rollback considerations.

This keeps the bead graph self-contained so future execution does not require referring back to prior planning documents.","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:32:43Z"}]}
 {"id":"wa-2xh0","title":"FTUI-01.2 Define wa target architecture ring map (core/runtime/widgets/tests)","description":"## Background\nfrankentui uses a ringed architecture with strict dependency direction. wa needs an explicit equivalent mapping to prevent cyclic regressions during migration.\n\n## Deliverables\n- ring map for wa modules and feature flags\n- ownership matrix for terminal/session/render/input responsibilities\n- boundary rules for where ftui-specific code can live\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- architecture map is committed and referenced by downstream tasks\n- no ambiguous ownership remains for terminal output and event loops.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PinkMountain","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:07:32.333916727Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-09T00:49:13.424086710Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T00:49:13.423954384Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","ftui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-2xh0","depends_on_id":"wa-2dlw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:15:26.869751474Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"},{"issue_id":"wa-2xh0","depends_on_id":"wa-p85q","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:07:32.346992513Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"}]}
 {"id":"wa-2y1y","title":"FTUI-05.3.a Panes view filter/selection parity checklist","description":"## Background
Panes view is operationally critical; filter/selection behavior must remain predictable through migration.

## Deliverables
- parity checklist for ordering, filtering, selection persistence, bookmarks, and actions
- edge-case matrix for large pane sets, rapid updates, and stale metadata
- traceability links to adapter fixtures and view-level tests

## Acceptance Criteria
- checklist confirms parity under nominal and stress-like update conditions
- discrepancies include severity, rationale, and remediation owner
- evidence includes deterministic test output and concise logs that enable quick triage.,","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:14:18.819967318Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-08T21:30:41.242341769Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","subtask","views"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-2y1y","depends_on_id":"wa-2i6m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:25:04.560830507Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"},{"issue_id":"wa-2y1y","depends_on_id":"wa-dbxt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:14:18.838907184Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"}]}
 {"id":"wa-2yhi","title":"FTUI-06.4.a Text-input editing edge-case matrix","description":"## Background
Text input semantics must remain reliable across filters/search fields and constrained terminal widths.

## Deliverables
- edge-case matrix: cursor movement, word jumps, deletion variants, paste, focus changes, narrow-width behavior
- expected outcomes per editing command and keybinding variant
- test hooks/assertions reusable by input widget tasks

## Acceptance Criteria
- matrix covers all supported editing paths and known regressions
- tests validate behavior deterministically across redraw/refresh cycles
- logging output for failures pinpoints command, cursor state, and text buffer state.,","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:14:34.910842456Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-08T21:31:31.555674478Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","input","subtask"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-2yhi","depends_on_id":"wa-1s9k","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:14:34.927309836Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"},{"issue_id":"wa-2yhi","depends_on_id":"wa-2y1y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:25:29.339610800Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"}]}
-{"id":"wa-2zd7","title":"[EPIC] FTUI-06 Input, Actions, and Interaction Semantics Migration","description":"## Purpose
Migrate interaction model (keybindings, command execution handoff, dialogs, focus behavior) to ftui.

## Why
Even perfect rendering is insufficient if keyboard flows, command actions, and accessibility regress.

## Focus
- input parity map
- command handoff/resume transitions
- modal interactions and filters
- accessibility and focus traversal behavior","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:07:20.799867579Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-08T20:26:23.815193884Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","input","migration"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-2zd7","depends_on_id":"wa-1brb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:15:01.924444723Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"},{"issue_id":"wa-2zd7","depends_on_id":"wa-1ssn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:15:04.341033836Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"},{"issue_id":"wa-2zd7","depends_on_id":"wa-2wed","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:07:20.829941188Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"}],"comments":[{"id":273,"issue_id":"wa-2zd7","author":"GrayHarbor","text":"### Execution Rationale (FTUI-06)
FTUI-06 preserves keyboard-first operator workflows and command handoff behavior, which are critical to daily usability.","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:26:23Z"}]}
+{"id":"wa-2zd7","title":"[EPIC] FTUI-06 Input, Actions, and Interaction Semantics Migration","description":"## Purpose
Migrate interaction model (keybindings, command execution handoff, dialogs, focus behavior) to ftui.

## Why
Even perfect rendering is insufficient if keyboard flows, command actions, and accessibility regress.

## Focus
- input parity map
- command handoff/resume transitions
- modal interactions and filters

 {"id":"wa-35l","title":"wa robot workflow list: discover available workflows","description":"# wa robot workflow list

## Purpose
Allow agents to discover what workflows are available and their requirements.

## Command Interface
```bash
# List all workflows
wa robot workflow list

# Filter by trigger type
wa robot workflow list --trigger=event

# Include detailed descriptions
wa robot workflow list --verbose

# Filter by enabled status
wa robot workflow list --enabled

# JSON output (default in robot mode)
wa robot workflow list --json
```

## JSON Output Schema
```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "command": "workflow.list",
  "data": {
    "workflows": [
      {
        "name": "handle_compaction",
        "description": "Re-inject context after agent compaction",
        "enabled": true,
        "trigger_event_types": ["session.compaction"],
        "trigger_rule_ids": ["compaction.detected"],
        "agent_types": ["codex", "claude_code", "gemini"],
        "step_count": 5,
        "parameters": [],
        "requires_pane": true,
        "requires_approval": false,
        "can_abort": true,
        "destructive": false,
        "dependencies": []
      }
    ],
    "total": 5,
    "enabled_count": 4
  }
}
```

## Use Cases
1. **Discovery**: New agent learns what automations are available
2. **Capability check**: Agent verifies workflow exists before calling
3. **Documentation**: Generate help text from workflow metadata
4. **Debugging**: See what workflows could handle an event

## Implementation Notes
- Load workflow definitions from WorkflowRegistry
- Include both built-in and custom workflows
- Mark workflows with missing dependencies
- Filter by trigger type, enabled status

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_list_all_workflows() {
    let registry = setup_test_registry();
    let output = execute_robot(&["workflow", "list"]);
    
    assert!(output["ok"].as_bool().unwrap());
    let workflows = output["data"]["workflows"].as_array().unwrap();
    assert!(workflows.len() > 0);
    
    // Verify required fields present
    let wf = &workflows[0];
    assert!(wf["name"].is_string());
    assert!(wf["enabled"].is_boolean());
}

#[test]
fn test_list_filter_by_trigger() {
    let output = execute_robot(&["workflow", "list", "--trigger=event"]);
    
    let workflows = output["data"]["workflows"].as_array().unwrap();
    for wf in workflows {
        assert!(wf["trigger_event_types"].as_array().unwrap().len() > 0);
    }
}

#[test]
fn test_list_filter_enabled_only() {
    let output = execute_robot(&["workflow", "list", "--enabled"]);
    
    let workflows = output["data"]["workflows"].as_array().unwrap();
    for wf in workflows {
        assert!(wf["enabled"].as_bool().unwrap());
    }
}

#[test]
fn test_list_json_schema_validation() {
    let output = execute_robot(&["workflow", "list"]);
    validate_against_schema(&output, "wa-robot-workflow-list.json");
}

#[test]
fn test_list_verbose_includes_descriptions() {
    let output = execute_robot(&["workflow", "list", "--verbose"]);
    
    let workflows = output["data"]["workflows"].as_array().unwrap();
    for wf in workflows {
        assert!(wf["description"].is_string());
        assert!(!wf["description"].as_str().unwrap().is_empty());
    }
}
```

### E2E Test
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# e2e_workflow_list.sh
set -euo pipefail
LOG="${ARTIFACT_DIR:-/tmp}/workflow_list.log"
log() { echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] $*" | tee -a "$LOG"; }

log "=== Workflow List E2E ==="

# 1. Basic list
log "Testing basic list..."
LIST=$(wa robot workflow list)
log "Result: $LIST"
echo "$LIST" | jq -e '.ok == true' || { log "FAIL: ok not true"; exit 1; }
echo "$LIST" | jq -e '.data.workflows | length > 0' || { log "FAIL: no workflows"; exit 1; }

# 2. Filter by trigger
log "Testing trigger filter..."
FILTERED=$(wa robot workflow list --trigger=event)
log "Filtered: $FILTERED"
echo "$FILTERED" | jq -e '.ok == true' || { log "FAIL: filter failed"; exit 1; }

# 3. Verify counts
log "Verifying counts..."
TOTAL=$(echo "$LIST" | jq '.data.total')
ENABLED=$(echo "$LIST" | jq '.data.enabled_count')
log "Total: $TOTAL, Enabled: $ENABLED"
[ "$ENABLED" -le "$TOTAL" ] || { log "FAIL: enabled > total"; exit 1; }

log "=== PASS: workflow_list ==="
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Lists all registered workflows
- [ ] Includes trigger and agent type info
- [ ] Shows dependency requirements
- [ ] --trigger filter works
- [ ] --enabled filter works
- [ ] --verbose includes full descriptions
- [ ] JSON validates against wa-robot-workflow-list.json schema
- [ ] Unit tests pass
- [ ] E2E test passes with detailed logging

 {"id":"wa-37x","title":"[EPIC] Reliability Hardening: Circuit Breakers, Chaos Testing, Graceful Degradation","description":"# [EPIC] Reliability Hardening

## Mission
Make wa **resilient to failures** at every level—external services, internal components, and edge cases.

## Why This Matters
wa controls terminal interactions. Failures must be handled gracefully:
- Browser automation fails → do not spam retries
- WezTerm CLI hangs → do not block forever
- DB corrupts → continue observing, degrade gracefully

Reliability is a trust multiplier. Users need confidence that wa will not make things worse when something goes wrong.

## Components

### 1. Circuit Breaker Pattern
Stop hammering failed services:
```
Service: browser_auth
Status: OPEN (tripped 3 failures)
Last failure: 2026-01-18T14:32:01 (timeout)
Retry after: 2026-01-18T14:37:01 (5 min cooldown)
```

Circuit breakers for:
- Browser automation (auth flows)
- `caut` CLI wrapper
- WezTerm CLI (if consistently failing)
- External webhooks

### 2. Watchdog/Heartbeat System
Monitor wa's own health:
- Heartbeat thread checks main loop liveness
- Deadlock detection (no progress for N seconds)
- Auto-restart on unrecoverable hang
- Crash report with diagnostic context

### 3. Chaos Testing Harness
Inject failures to prove resilience:
- Kill WezTerm mid-capture
- Corrupt DB file
- Simulate network timeouts
- Exhaust disk space
- Fill queues to capacity

Chaos tests validate:
- Data integrity after failures
- Graceful degradation behavior
- Recovery procedures work

### 4. Retry with Exponential Backoff
Standardized retry policy:
- Initial: 100ms
- Max: 30s
- Backoff factor: 2x
- Jitter: ±10%
- Max attempts: configurable

Applied to:
- WezTerm CLI calls
- DB writes (transient lock conflicts)
- External service calls

### 5. Graceful Degradation Modes
When components fail, continue with reduced functionality:
- DB read-only → continue observing, queue writes
- Pattern engine error → skip detection, keep ingesting
- Workflow failure → pause workflow, keep observing
- WezTerm CLI unavailable → retry with backoff, surface status

## Observability
All reliability mechanisms emit structured logs:
- Circuit state changes
- Retry attempts and outcomes
- Degradation mode transitions
- Watchdog warnings

## Testing
- Unit tests: Circuit breaker state machine
- Integration tests: Retry behavior under failure injection
- Chaos tests: System survives failure scenarios
- Load tests: Behavior under resource exhaustion

## Success Criteria
- Circuit breakers prevent retry storms
- Watchdog detects and recovers from hangs
- Chaos tests pass for critical failure scenarios
- Graceful degradation maintains core observability

## Acceptance Criteria
- Circuit breakers, retries, and watchdogs prevent runaway failures.
- Graceful degradation keeps core commands usable.
- Chaos harness runs without crashes and produces actionable logs.

 {"id":"wa-37x.1","title":"Circuit breaker infrastructure: state machine, cooldowns, and status reporting","description":"
# Circuit Breaker Infrastructure

## Purpose
Implement a generic circuit breaker pattern to prevent retry storms when services fail.

## State Machine
```
     failures < threshold
CLOSED ◄────────────────────────► OPEN
  │                                  │
  │ failure >= threshold             │ cooldown elapsed
  └──────────────────►               ▼
                              HALF-OPEN
                                  │
                    success → CLOSED
                    failure → OPEN
```

## Implementation
```rust
pub struct CircuitBreaker {
    name: String,
    state: CircuitState,
    failure_count: u32,
    failure_threshold: u32,
    cooldown: Duration,
    last_failure: Option<Instant>,
}

impl CircuitBreaker {
    pub fn call<T>(&self, f: impl FnOnce() -> Result<T>) -> Result<T, CircuitError>;
    pub fn record_success(&mut self);
    pub fn record_failure(&mut self, error: &Error);
    pub fn status(&self) -> CircuitStatus;
}
```

## Circuit Breakers to Implement
- browser_auth: For Playwright auth flows
- caut_cli: For account usage queries
- wezterm_cli: For pane operations
- webhook: For notification delivery

## Status Reporting
```
$ wa doctor --circuits

Circuit Breaker Status:
  browser_auth: CLOSED (healthy)
  caut_cli:     OPEN (3 failures, retry in 4m32s)
  wezterm_cli:  CLOSED (healthy)
  webhook:      HALF-OPEN (testing...)
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CircuitBreaker struct with state machine
- [ ] Configurable thresholds and cooldowns
- [ ] Status visible in wa doctor
- [ ] Structured logging on state changes

## Testing
- Unit tests for breaker/backoff logic and state transitions.
- Integration tests simulating IO failures and recovery paths.
- Chaos harness scenarios with verbose logs and recovery assertions.

 {"id":"wa-3g9.3","title":"Connection pooling for WezTerm: reuse mux connections when available","description":"
# Connection Pooling for WezTerm

## Purpose
Reduce overhead of WezTerm CLI calls by pooling connections to the mux server.

## Current Behavior
Each WezTerm CLI call spawns a new process, connects to mux, executes, and disconnects.

## Proposed Behavior
When using vendored mode (direct mux protocol):
```rust
pub struct WezTermPool {
    connections: Vec<MuxConnection>,
    max_size: usize,
    idle_timeout: Duration,
}

impl WezTermPool {
    pub async fn execute<T>(&self, cmd: MuxCommand) -> Result<T> {
        let conn = self.acquire().await?;
        let result = conn.execute(cmd).await;
        self.release(conn);
        result
    }
}
```

## Benefits
- Avoid process spawn overhead (~10ms per call)
- Reduce socket churn
- Enable connection keepalive
- Batch multiple operations efficiently

## Prerequisites
- Requires vendored mode (wa-nu4.4.1)
- Direct mux protocol access

## Fallback
CLI mode: No pooling possible, but can batch commands.

## Benchmark Targets
- With pooling: ~1ms per operation
- Without pooling: ~10-20ms per operation
- 10x improvement for high-frequency operations

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Connection pool implemented for vendored mode
- [ ] Idle connection cleanup
- [ ] Graceful fallback for CLI mode
- [ ] Benchmark proves improvement

## Testing
- Criterion benchmarks with budgets and baseline comparison.
- Microbenchmarks for the specific optimization.
- CI regression guard with perf logs.

 {"id":"wa-3g9.4","title":"Incremental FTS sync: avoid full reindex, track per-pane progress","description":"
# Incremental FTS Sync

## Purpose
Ensure FTS index only processes new content, avoiding expensive full reindex operations.

## Current Challenge
On restart, wa might need to determine what's already indexed and what's new.

## Solution
```rust
pub struct FtsIndexState {
    /// Last indexed segment seq per pane
    per_pane_progress: HashMap<PaneId, SegmentSeq>,
    /// Global index version for schema changes
    index_version: u32,
}
```

## Sync Process
1. On startup: Load `per_pane_progress` from DB
2. For each pane: Query segments where `seq > last_indexed_seq`
3. Index only new segments
4. Update progress after successful batch

## Batching Strategy
- Batch size: 100 segments or 1MB text
- Commit progress after each batch
- Resume from last batch on crash

## Performance Impact
- Full reindex: O(total segments)
- Incremental: O(new segments)
- Startup overhead: O(1) - just load progress

## Recovery
If index corrupt or schema changed:
- Detect via `index_version` mismatch
- Trigger full rebuild
- Show progress indicator

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Per-pane progress tracked in DB
- [ ] Only new segments indexed on restart
- [ ] Batch processing with progress commits
- [ ] Full rebuild triggered on version mismatch
- [ ] Benchmark shows startup improvement

## Testing
- Criterion benchmarks with budgets and baseline comparison.
- Microbenchmarks for the specific optimization.
- CI regression guard with perf logs.

 {"id":"wa-3g9.5","title":"Performance benchmarks and budgets: Criterion suite with regression guards","description":"# Performance benchmarks and budgets (Criterion)

## Goal
Establish benchmarks for all performance-critical paths and prevent regressions with explicit, documented budgets.

This system sits on the hot path of “every command the user runs” (watch loop, pattern matching, policy checks). Performance regressions are user-visible.

## Benchmark suite (Criterion)
Benchmarks must target *real* hot paths and be runnable locally:

```rust
// benches/pattern_engine.rs
criterion_group!(
    pattern_benchmarks,
    bench_bloom_check,
    bench_aho_corasick_match,
    bench_full_pattern_pipeline,
);

// benches/ingest.rs
criterion_group!(
    ingest_benchmarks,
    bench_delta_extraction,
    bench_segment_persistence,
    bench_fts_indexing,
);
```

## What we benchmark (minimum set)
- Quick reject / prefilter:
  - `memchr`/bloom/no-match fast paths
  - worst-case “near match but reject” path
- Pattern pipeline:
  - no-hit text (must be very fast)
  - one-hit typical text
  - extraction path with captures
- Ingest/deltas:
  - delta extraction on typical scrollback
  - gap detection path
- Storage:
  - write queue throughput (bounded queue)
  - FTS indexing cost for typical segment sizes
- Query surfaces:
  - typical FTS query latency (scoped)
  - worst-case query guardrails (bounded)

## Performance budgets (absolute ceilings)
Budgets are set as “this must feel snappy to users” ceilings. Exact numbers may be tuned after first implementation, but budgets must exist before optimization work is called done.

| Operation | Budget (target ceiling) | Notes |
|-----------|--------------------------|-------|
| Bloom filter check | < 1µs | hot path prefilter |
| Pattern match (no hit) | < 10µs | must be cheap for most text |
| Pattern match (with hit) | < 100µs | includes extraction |
| Delta extraction | < 1ms | per pane per tick typical |
| FTS insert (single) | < 5ms | typical segment size |
| FTS query (typical) | < 50ms | scoped query; “feels instant” |
| Startup (no patterns) | < 100ms | CLI responsiveness |

## Regression detection strategy (CI realities)
Perf measurement is noisy on shared runners. The plan must be robust:

- Local dev loop:
  - `cargo bench` is the source of truth for tuning.
  - Provide a short “budget check” summary line per benchmark so developers can self-serve.

- CI loop (two-tier):
  1) **PR job (warn-only by default)**
     - Run benches and upload `criterion` output + a short summary artifact.
     - Fail only on catastrophic regressions (e.g., >2x slower OR exceeding a hard ceiling by a large margin).
  2) **Main/perf job (enforce budgets)**
     - Prefer a stable environment (self-hosted runner if available).
     - Enforce budgets strictly and record historical trend artifacts.

- Baselines:
  - Store a baseline artifact from main (or a tagged commit) and compare PR results against it.
  - Comparisons must ignore obviously-noisy fields and focus on timing deltas.

## Reporting
Example (desired UX):
```
$ cargo bench --bench pattern_engine

bloom_check         time:   [0.8 µs 0.9 µs 1.0 µs]  ✓ (budget: 1µs)
aho_corasick_match  time:   [8.2 µs 8.5 µs 8.8 µs]  ✓ (budget: 10µs)
full_pipeline       time:   [45 µs 48 µs 51 µs]     ✓ (budget: 100µs)
```

On regression, the report must include:
- which benchmark regressed
- old vs new timings
- which budget was exceeded (if any)
- where to find the artifacts (CI URL/path)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Criterion benchmarks exist for the minimum hot paths listed above.
- [ ] Budgets are documented and checked by an automated “budget check” step.
- [ ] CI produces artifacts for every run (raw criterion + summarized results).
- [ ] CI regression detection works (warn-only on PR; enforced on stable perf job).

## Testing
- Benchmark harness self-checks:
  - benches compile and run on supported platforms.
  - the budget-checking logic is covered by unit tests (parsing/threshold evaluation).
- CI regression guard tests:
  - include a small synthetic “known regression” fixture in test mode to verify:
    - the budget checker fails when it should
    - output includes the expected summary fields","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"JadeFox","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T17:54:35.705060335Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-08T08:39:30.156551521Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T08:39:30.156476833Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-perf","area-tests","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-3g9.5","depends_on_id":"wa-3g9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:25Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-3g9.5","depends_on_id":"wa-3g9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:25Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-3g9.5","depends_on_id":"wa-3g9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:25Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-3g9.5","depends_on_id":"wa-3g9.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:25Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"wa-3gsu","title":"FTUI-03.2.b Route subprocess output through PTY capture and sanitized forwarding","description":"## Background
Subprocess output can violate ownership/order guarantees unless it is normalized before presentation.

## Deliverables
- subprocess forwarding contract (sanitization, ordering, backpressure, redaction boundaries)
- implementation/test plan for integrating subprocess output into the one-writer sink
- diagnostics for dropped/reordered/blocked output scenarios

## Acceptance Criteria
- forwarding behavior is deterministic under bursty and interleaved output
- tests cover nominal and failure conditions with reproducible artifacts
- logs clearly separate subprocess-origin issues from UI/runtime issues for fast triage.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:14:08.937970098Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-08T21:31:40.970633651Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","one-writer","subtask"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-3gsu","depends_on_id":"wa-1uqi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:24:40.575896411Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"},{"issue_id":"wa-3gsu","depends_on_id":"wa-3cso","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:14:08.956003109Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"}]}
 {"id":"wa-3kal","title":"FTUI-04.2 Build adapter layer from QueryClient data to ftui view models","description":"## Background\nCurrent view structs are ratatui-oriented. We need explicit adapters that isolate rendering from backend schema evolution.\n\n## Deliverables\n- mapping modules for pane/event/triage/history/search/workflow views\n- normalization and redaction-safe formatting rules\n- unit tests on adapter transformations\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- adapters produce deterministic, render-ready view models\n- schema evolution does not require direct widget rewrites.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PinkMountain","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:08:00.269912667Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-09T01:25:54.877233043Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T01:25:54.877099585Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["adapter","ftui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-3kal","depends_on_id":"wa-1ssn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:08:00.285785871Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"},{"issue_id":"wa-3kal","depends_on_id":"wa-5htt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:16:49.969223411Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"},{"issue_id":"wa-3kal","depends_on_id":"wa-8q4e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:16:54.789966822Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"}]}
 {"id":"wa-3mus","title":"FTUI-08.3.a Compatibility runbook template per terminal/mux environment","description":"## Background
Compatibility certification must be repeatable across terminal/mux environments with uniform evidence quality.

## Deliverables
- runbook template defining environment metadata, test scope, expected outcomes, and failure policy
- required evidence checklist (logs, snapshots, command outputs, known limitations)
- comparison rubric for pass/conditional-pass/fail decisions

## Acceptance Criteria
- template can be reused for each target environment without interpretation drift
- evidence requirements explicitly include unit/E2E references and logging expectations
- resulting reports are concise, actionable, and suitable for release gating.,","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:14:42.357592844Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-08T21:31:10.368068104Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["compat","ftui","subtask"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-3mus","depends_on_id":"wa-308u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:25:43.785878589Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"},{"issue_id":"wa-3mus","depends_on_id":"wa-e69a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:14:42.374372734Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"}]}
